steps.title: Solve the system by substitution
steps.content: Substitute $y=19$ into the first equation: $$19=4x-9.$$ Solve: $4x=28$, so $x=7$.

steps.title: Find the matching ordered pair
steps.content: The ordered pair is $(7,19)$. The answer is (B) $(7,19)$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. It's given by the second equation in the system that $y=19$. Substituting $19$ for $y$ in the first equation yields $19=4x-9$. Adding $9$ to both sides of this equation yields $28=4x$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $4$ yields $7=x$. Therefore, since $x=7$ and $y=19$, the solution $\left(x,y\right)$ to the given system of equations is $\left(7,19\right)$.
